title: drosera nitidula var. leucostigma
intro: Drosera nitidula var. leucostigma, also known as the white-spotted sundew, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the Droseraceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, specifically in the Alps and the Carpathian Mountains. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce inflammation, making it useful in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and arthritis. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide health benefits such as improved digestion and reduced symptoms of respiratory issues.
